P.O. Box 5175 
Sacramento, Ca. 
95817 
Dear Mr. Grabow: 
EO'.1UND G. 8 RDi:JiJ JR .• Gov2rf'Or 
December 16, 1975 
Re: 
Request for Opinion 
No. 75-211 
Thank you for your letter of December I, 1975, requesting 
an opinion with respect to the reporting requirements for lobby-
ists under the Political Reform Act. 
Since your request does not 
raise a novel question involving interpretation of the Act, no 
formal opinion will be issued. 
I hope, however, that the follow-
ing comments will be helpful. 
As a lobbyist, you are required to report all transactions 
in connection with lobbying activities and conduct all such trans-
actions through a lobbyist account. 
Government Code Section 86106 
and 86107. 
Government Code Section 86106 also provides that all 
gifts made by lobbyists to elective state officials, legislative 
officials or agency officials are deemed to be in connection with 
lobbying activities. 
Accordingly, you may continue to make gifts 
of a pound of butter to such officials, but these gifts must be 
transacted through the lobbyist account and reported on your 
lobbyist periodic report. 
If you wish to pay for these gifts with 
your personal funds, you may do so by depositing the personal funds 
into your lobbyist account. 
If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request, you 
may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321, a copy of 
which is attached hereto. 
If you have any further questions, 
please contact Ted Prim, an attorney with the Commission staff. 
DHL:pvp 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
-)

Daniel H. Lowenstein 
Chairman, for the Commission

sections.question: On January 11, 1973, 1974, and 1975, I presented the Senators and Assemblyman with a pound of Butter. I also presented the Secretaries of the Governor's Cabinet, the Governor and the Greeting Secretary with a pound of Butter. I also presented the Sacramento County Board of Supervisors with a pound of Butter. I also presented 1 pound of Butter to other individuals. The value of each pound of Butter was less than $1.00. The total number of pounds of Butter was 210. I paid for this Butter personally in celebration of my Birthday, January 11, 1898. Do you approve of my continuing this on Jan. 11, 1976?
sections.conclusion: As a lobbyist, you are required to report all transactions in connection with lobbying activities and conduct all such transactions through a lobbyist account. Government Code Section 86106 and 86107. Government Code Section 86106 also provides that all gifts made by lobbyists to elective state officials, legislative officials or agency officials are deemed to be in connection with lobbying activities. Accordingly, you may continue to make gifts of a pound of butter to such officials, but these gifts must be transacted through the lobbyist account and reported on your lobbyist periodic report. If you wish to pay for these gifts with your personal funds, you may do so by depositing the personal funds into your lobbyist account.
sections.question_synthetic: As a registered lobbyist, may I continue to give small gifts of butter (under $1 value) to state officials, legislators, and county supervisors on my birthday if I pay for them personally?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Lobbyists may give gifts to state officials, but all such gifts are deemed to be in connection with lobbying activities and must be reported. Gifts must be transacted through and reported via the lobbyist account, though personal funds may be deposited into the account for this purpose.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Request for Opinion No. 75-211; FPPC declined to issue formal opinion citing lack of novel legal question, but provided advisory comments. Document includes both incoming request letter and FPPC response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: On January 11, 1973, 1974, and 1975, I presented the Senators and Assemblyman with a pound of Butter. I also presented the Secretaries of the Governor's Cabinet, the Governor and the Greeting Secretary with a pound of Butter. I also presented the Sacramento County Board of Supervisors with a pound of Butter. I also presented 1 pound of Butter to other individuals. The value of each pound of Butter was less than $1.00. The total number of pounds of Butter was 210. I paid for this Butter personally in celebration of my Birthday, January 11, 1898. Do you approve of my continuing this on Jan. 11, 1976?

CONCLUSION: As a lobbyist, you are required to report all transactions in connection with lobbying activities and conduct all such transactions through a lobbyist account. Government Code Section 86106 and 86107. Government Code Section 86106 also provides that all gifts made by lobbyists to elective state officials, legislative officials or agency officials are deemed to be in connection with lobbying activities. Accordingly, you may continue to make gifts of a pound of butter to such officials, but these gifts must be transacted through the lobbyist account and reported on your lobbyist periodic report. If you wish to pay for these gifts with your personal funds, you may do so by depositing the personal funds into your lobbyist account.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a lobbyist's question about whether he may continue to give small butter gifts to state officials and legislators. The Commission advises that while such gifts are permissible, they must be reported as lobbying-related transactions through the lobbyist account.
